title: Vouacapoua anthelmia
intro: Vouacapoua anthelmia is a plant species commonly known as the "Sausage tree" due to its unique sausage-shaped fruit. The health benefits of Vouacapoua anthelmia include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Vouacapoua anthelmia is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and other health issues.
taxonomy: Vouacapoua anthelmia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. It is a legume native to tropical Africa. The plant's classification further includes being a member of the genus Vouacapoua and the subfamily Faboideae. This classification places Vouacapoua anthelmia within the larger group of legumes that are characterized by their fruit pods and compound leaves. The specific classification of Vouacapoua anthelmia reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Fabaceae family.
